macro_context.summary: April 2026 macro regime: stagflationary crosscurrents dominate. The Iran war has pushed Brent crude to ~$101-111/bbl (+$35-47 YoY), reigniting energy inflation and freezing the Fed at 3.50-3.75% despite growth softening. The 10Y UST sits at 4.35%, credit spreads are near 25-year tights (IG OAS ~80 bps, HY ~285 bps), and the DXY has corrected ~10% from its 2025 peak to ~98-99 — weakening against EUR (~1.17) and CHF — which is materially positive for Swiss and European assets in USD terms. AI hyperscaler capex is surging ($600-630B aggregate 2026E, +36-62% YoY), keeping the power/grid/semicap complex structurally bid, but the cycle is maturing and vulnerable to any demand digestion signal. Central bank divergence is acute: BOJ tightening to 0.75% (hawkish dissenters want 1.0%), ECB debating hikes from 2.00%, BOE frozen at 3.75%. Equity factor leadership has rotated toward value/quality in Europe and small-cap value showing early reversal after years of growth dominance; defensives have re-rated as rate-cut expectations were crushed.

key_drivers.level: 10Y UST 4.35%; Fed funds 3.50-3.75%; Core PCE 2.8%; implied real rate ~+1.5-1.6%
key_drivers.implication: Positive real rates constrain long-duration assets and pre-revenue names (OKLO, SMR, NuScale). Swiss names insulated if CHF holds. High-yielders (SREN, SCHD, GLIFX) competitive vs bonds only if spreads stay tight. Any recession pivot instantly re-rates duration.

key_drivers.level: DXY ~98-99, down ~10% from 2025 highs; EUR/USD ~1.17; USD/CHF ~0.87; USD/CAD bearish
key_drivers.implication: USD weakness is a structural tailwind for EUR/CHF-denominated assets in USD terms — Swiss names (GIVN, ROG, NESN, TECN, SREN, SGSN), ASM.AS (EUR), PRY.MI (EUR), RR.L (GBP) all benefit from translation. SU (CAD) and TGB (CAD) get a modest lift. Conversely, XOM/PSIX/AMSC lose FX tailwind.

key_drivers.level: Brent ~$101-106/bbl (late April 2026); WTI ~$98-102; +$35-47/bbl YoY driven by Iran war Hormuz risk
key_drivers.implication: Strongly positive for SU, XOM, XLE, TNK (tanker rates/geopolitical premium), TE.PA (LNG/energy infra). Moderately positive for HGER (commodity ETF). Negative for European industrials (energy cost), airlines (not on list). Inflationary for broader economy, keeping central banks on hold and compressing equity multiples.

key_drivers.level: GDP growth slowing to 4.5% in 2026 from ~5% in 2025; property sector down 40-50% from peak but stabilizing; modest stimulus signaled
key_drivers.implication: Negative for pure China-cyclical commodity plays. Mixed for copper (TGB): structural long-term demand intact but near-term softness. ASM.AS China revenue at risk from export controls. HGER copper component under modest pressure. Uranium (CCJ) relatively insulated — China nuclear build is a multi-decade state program.

key_drivers.level: Hyperscaler 2026E capex $600-630B (+36-62% YoY); ~75% AI infrastructure; power demand 'speed to power' critical constraint
key_drivers.implication: Strongest structural tailwind for AMSC, PSIX, HUBB (grid/power solutions), ASM.AS (ALD/semicap), SNDK (NAND cycle). Nuclear as baseload for data centers: BWXT, OKLO, SMR, CCJ, ASY.PA. The capex growth rate is decelerating from 73% to ~36% — still expanding but rate of change softening, which matters for momentum names.

key_drivers.level: Iran war driving Hormuz risk; Russia/Ukraine frozen; US tariffs creating supply chain fragmentation; European rearmament accelerating
key_drivers.implication: European defense a structural winner: RR.L, EXA.PA (naval drones/defense robotics), ASY.PA (nuclear/defense infra). Tanker rates supported by Red Sea/Hormuz: TNK benefits. Energy security premium supports nuclear (CCJ, OKLO, SMR, BWXT) and LNG (TE.PA). US tariff risk adds friction for ASM.AS (Dutch semicap), PRY.MI (cable imports).

executive_summary: OVER-REPRESENTED: Nuclear/SMR cluster (OKLO, SMR, BWXT, CCJ, ASY.PA = 15% of list) creates concentration risk precisely when the theme is most crowded; pre-revenue names OKLO and SMR require long time horizons incompatible with near-term macro uncertainty.

OVER-REPRESENTED: US power-grid/AI capex cyclicals (AMSC, PSIX, HUBB, SNDK) are deeply correlated to the same AI capex cycle that is already decelerating in growth rate; all four are 'Premium Priced' or near-consensus-target with limited new upside.

UNDER-REPRESENTED: Gold/precious metals exposure is essentially zero despite gold at $4,838/oz (near all-time highs, up ~14% YTD) — with real rates likely to fall if growth rolls over, gold miners or a GLD-type position is a conspicuous gap as a macro hedge.
UNDER-REPRESENTED: EM ex-China and healthcare beyond Roche — only ROG.SW and TECN.SW represent healthcare; no EM equity, no emerging Asia fintech, no healthcare services/managed care.

MOST ACTIONABLE NOW (all-three-angle conviction): GIVN.SW (47% upside, below-historical P/E, CHF deflation hedge, buy signal active), RR.L (22% upside, turnaround validated, aerospace recovery structural), PSIX (36% upside, clean AI power-grid play at 15x P/E, buy signal active), TECN.SW (62% upside, biopharma recovery, 11x EV/EBITDA, buy signal active), SU (43% upside, oil at $101+ vs breakeven ~$50, buy signal active).

SHARP CONFLICTS TO WATCH: IRDM (trading 28% ABOVE consensus target — a hard sell/avoid even though the satellite moat thesis is structurally valid); PRY.MI (trading 10% above target despite copper input cost surge from oil-shock inflation); HUBB (at 33x P/E vs 23x historical average with only -1.7% upside — valuation stretched even with grid tailwind).

KEY MACRO RISKS TO WHOLE LIST: (1) Iran de-escalation would crash oil to $70s, cutting SU/XOM/TNK/XLE earnings, unwinding HGER gains, and removing the nuclear energy-security premium from OKLO/SMR; (2) Fed surprise hike would re-rate all long-duration and pre-revenue names; (3) AI capex digestion (any hyperscaler capex cut) would simultaneously hit AMSC, PSIX, HUBB, ASM.AS, SNDK — a cluster that is not as diversified as it looks.
